meta.title: 大法官的差異有多大？
meta.subtitle: 用貝氏階層模型處理小樣本比例與部分匯聚
meta.summary: 甲在 4 件參與案件中提出 3 件個別意見書，乙在 100 件中提出 40 件。75% 與 40% 可以描述已觀察的案件，卻不足以支持排名。本文用二項模型、後驗分布與部分匯聚，說明小樣本比例應如何估計與報告。

en.title: How Large Are the Differences Between Justices?
en.subtitle: Small-sample proportions and partial pooling in a Bayesian hierarchical model
en.summary: Justice A files a separate opinion in 3 of 4 participating cases; justice B does so in 40 of 100. The two proportions describe the observed cases, but they do not justify a ranking. This article uses a binomial model, posterior distributions, and partial pooling to show how small-sample proportions can be estimated and reported.

gelmanEtAl2013BayesianDataAnalysis.author: Andrew Gelman, John B. Carlin, Hal S. Stern, David B. Dunson, Aki Vehtari, and Donald B. Rubin
gelmanEtAl2013BayesianDataAnalysis.title: Bayesian Data Analysis
gelmanEtAl2013BayesianDataAnalysis.locator: Ch. 2, §2.1, pp.29-37（binomial likelihood, beta prior, posterior distribution）；Ch. 5, pp.101-104（hierarchical models, exchangeability, and the rat-tumor example）。
gelmanEtAl2013BayesianDataAnalysis.quote: Many statistical applications involve multiple parameters that can be regarded as related or connected in some way by the structure of the problem.

en.locator: Ch. 2 §2.1, pp.29-37 (the binomial likelihood, beta prior, and posterior distribution); Ch. 5, pp.101-104 (hierarchical models, exchangeability, and the rat-tumor example).
en.quote: Many statistical applications involve multiple parameters that can be regarded as related or connected in some way by the structure of the problem.

gelmanHillVehtari2020RegressionStories.author: Andrew Gelman, Jennifer Hill, and Aki Vehtari
gelmanHillVehtari2020RegressionStories.title: Regression and Other Stories
gelmanHillVehtari2020RegressionStories.locator: Ch. 4, §4.2, pp.50-52（estimates, standard errors, and intervals）；§4.4, pp.59-60（statistical significance, Type S and Type M errors）。
gelmanHillVehtari2020RegressionStories.quote: The confidence interval represents a range of values of a parameter or quantity of interest that are roughly consistent with the data, given the assumed sampling distribution.

en.locator: Ch. 4 §4.2, pp.50-52 (estimates, standard errors, and intervals); §4.4, pp.59-60 (statistical significance, Type S and Type M errors).
en.quote: The confidence interval represents a range of values of a parameter or quantity of interest that are roughly consistent with the data, given the assumed sampling distribution.

gelmanHillYajima2009MultipleComparisons.author: Andrew Gelman, Jennifer Hill, and Masanao Yajima
gelmanHillYajima2009MultipleComparisons.title: Why We (Usually) Don't Have to Worry About Multiple Comparisons
gelmanHillYajima2009MultipleComparisons.container: Journal of Research on Educational Effectiveness, 5(2), pp.189-211
gelmanHillYajima2009MultipleComparisons.locator: Abstract and discussion of multilevel modeling（partial pooling shifts estimates toward each other; conventional multiple-comparison procedures instead widen intervals or adjust p values）。
gelmanHillYajima2009MultipleComparisons.quote: Multilevel models perform partial pooling (shifting estimates toward each other).

en.locator: Abstract and discussion of multilevel modeling (partial pooling shifts estimates toward each other; conventional multiple-comparison procedures instead widen intervals or adjust p values).
en.quote: Multilevel models perform partial pooling (shifting estimates toward each other).

quiz.prompt: 甲在 4 件參與案件中有 3 件提出意見書，乙在 100 件中有 40 件提出。只看 75% 與 40%，最直接漏掉了什麼？
options: 兩個比例背後的案件數不同，因此估計的不確定性不同
乙一定比甲更常提出意見書
百分比不能用於司法資料
quiz.explain: 比例本身沒有錯，但 3/4 和 40/100 提供的資訊量差很多。任何比較都要同時報告案件數與不確定性。

quiz.prompt: 部分匯聚會怎麼處理只有少量案件的個別比例？
options: 直接刪除小樣本大法官
把所有人的比例改成全體平均
讓小樣本估計同時參考自己的資料與整體分布；案件數增加後，自己的資料會占更大權重
把每個比例都做顯著性檢定，只保留 p < 0.05 的人
quiz.explain: 部分匯聚不是抹平差異。它讓資料量少的估計保留較大的不確定性，並以群體資料提供有限的資訊；資料量多時，個別資料自然占較大權重。

quiz.prompt: 如果研究要比較大法官的立場，以下哪一項最不能直接拿來當作立場指標？
options: 事先定義、逐項編碼且經複核的表決方向
每案是否提出意見書，並清楚說明量的是發表意見書的傾向
意見書篇數，卻直接把它解讀成意識形態位置
依案件類型分層後的表決紀錄
quiz.explain: 意見書篇數可以描述發表行為，不能自行變成立場。統計模型只能處理已定義的結果變項，不能修補構念和指標之間的落差。

confidence-interval.oneLine: 一套會生出區間的規則。95% 是這套規則的長期命中率，不是眼前這一個區間的機率。
confidence-interval.example: Neyman 1937 自己問：對已經算出來的那一個區間，能不能說真值落在裡面的機率是 95%？他的答案是不能。

en.oneLine: A rule for producing intervals. The 95% is the rule's long-run hit rate, not the probability for the one interval in front of you.
en.example: Neyman asked it himself in 1937: of an interval already computed, may we say the true value lies inside it with probability 95%? His answer was no.
